summary: DHH reports that 37signals' cloud exit savings have exceeded original estimates, projecting over $10 million in savings over five years. The 2024 cloud bill dropped from $3.2 million to $1.3 million annually, with the remaining spend on AWS S3 set to end next summer when they migrate to Pure Storage. The $700K hardware investment was fully recouped during the transition year. He argues the industry's all-cloud-everything mentality has peaked and companies should do the math on their cloud spending.
key_points: Annual cloud spending dropped from $3.2M to $1.3M, with $700K in new hardware fully paid off from 2023 transition-year savings
The remaining $1.3M S3 spend will be replaced by an 18PB Pure Storage setup at roughly the same cost as one year of S3, saving another $4M over five years
No hidden operational costs materialized — the same team manages everything without needing to hire additional staff
The cloud still makes sense for early-stage companies and highly variable workloads, but established companies with substantial bills should run the numbers
37signals plans to fully delete their AWS account by summer 2025
money_quotes: For 2024, we've brought the cloud bill down from the original $3.2 million/year run rate to $1.3 million.
Think about that for a second. This is gear we expect to use for the next five, maybe even seven years! All paid off from savings accrued during the second half of 2023.
There were no hidden dragons of additional workload associated with the exit that required us to balloon the team, as some spectators speculated when we announced it.
The motto of the 2010s and early 2020s – all-cloud, everything, all the time – seems to finally have peaked. And thank heavens for that!
But as soon as the cloud bills start to become substantial, I think you owe it to yourself, your investors, and common business sense to at least do the math.
The potential savings from these answers can be shocking.
At 37signals, we're looking forward to literally deleting our AWS account come this summer.
key_insight: 37signals' cloud exit proved even more profitable than projected, saving over $10 million in five years while maintaining the same team size, challenging the industry assumption that cloud is always the right choice for established companies.
